name: New Brunswick Postal
description_short: New Brunswick Postal Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution established in 1937, serving government and public sector employees with $18.3M in assets.
description_long:
New Brunswick Postal Federal Credit Union was established in 1937 and is headquartered in Edison, New Jersey. As a federal credit union, it operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution tailored specifically for public sector employees and government workers. The organization has maintained its presence for over 85 years, building a loyal membership base in the New Jersey area.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking accounts (Share Draft accounts), savings accounts (Regular Savings and Money Market), certificates of deposit, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, and mortgage options. Members can access online banking, mobile app services, bill payment capabilities, and various financial calculators. The institution provides both traditional in-branch services and digital banking solutions for member convenience.

New Brunswick Postal distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on public sector employees, creating tailored financial solutions for a specific membership demographic. With over 2,200 members and $18.3 million in assets under stewardship, the credit union maintains a tight-knit community approach. The institution operates a single main branch at 19 Kilmer Road in Edison, NJ, with dedicated staff oversight and personalized member service rather than a sprawling branch network.

However, the credit union's small size presents both advantages and limitations. While members benefit from community-focused service and membership-based governance, the single-branch operation and lack of ATM network may limit accessibility compared to larger financial institutions. The lobby is currently closed according to the available information, and prospective members should verify current operating status and service availability before visiting or applying for membership.
services: Financial Calculators (mortgage, auto, credit card payoff)
pros: Member-owned credit union structure with democratic governance and profits returned to members
Competitive offerings including auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and credit cards
Mobile banking app available on both iOS and Android platforms
Online banking with bill pay functionality for member convenience
Established 1937 — over 85 years of financial stability and community trust
Specialized membership eligibility for government and public sector employees
Financial calculators available (mortgage, auto loan, credit card payoff) for planning
cons: Single branch location only (19 Kilmer Road, Edison, NJ) — severely limited physical accessibility
Zero ATM network according to available data — no direct ATM access for withdrawals
Very small asset base ($18.3M) compared to larger financial institutions — may limit product depth and competitive rates
Lobby currently closed — current operational status unclear and requires verification
Limited membership base (2,200 members) restricts economies of scale and product innovation
best_for: Government employees and public sector workers in the New Jersey area eligible for membership
Members seeking community-focused, member-owned banking with personalized service
Individuals willing to use online/mobile banking due to single physical branch limitation
diagnosis: New Brunswick Postal Federal Credit Union is best for government and public sector employees in New Jersey seeking member-owned banking with personalized service. The primary caveat is the single-branch operation with no ATM network, making it impractical for members requiring physical accessibility beyond the Edison location or frequent cash withdrawals.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
